response:
THE FINAL WEIGHT OF THE FRAME, $ W_f $, CAN BE EXPRESSED AS $ W_f = W \times (1 - 0.02)^n $, WHERE $ W $ IS THE BASE WEIGHT OF THE FRAME AND $ N $ IS THE NUMBER OF ADVANCED COMPONENTS ADDED. THIS EQUATION ACCOUNTS FOR THE 2% WEIGHT REDUCTION PER COMPONENT.

TO ACHIEVE A TOTAL REDUCTION OF ENERGY EXPENDITURE BY 25%, THE FUNCTION $ E(n) = 1 - (1 - 0.02)^n $ MUST EQUAL OR EXCEED 0.25. SOLVING $ 1 - (0.98)^n \geq 0.25 $, WE GET $ (0.98)^n \leq 0.75 $. TAKING THE NATURAL LOGARITHM OF BOTH SIDES, $ n \times \ln(0.98) \leq \ln(0.75) $. SINCE $ \ln(0.98) $ IS NEGATIVE, DIVIDING BOTH SIDES BY IT REVERSES THE INEQUALITY: $ n \geq \ln(0.75) / \ln(0.98) $. CALCULATING THIS GIVES $ N \geq 14.2 $. THUS, THE MINIMUM NUMBER OF COMPONENTS REQUIRED IS 15.

THE CLOCK TIKS ON AS ALEX CONSIDERS THE IMPACT OF EACH COMPONENT. THE CLOCK IS A REMINDER OF THE TIME PRESSURE TO MAKE THE RIGHT CHOICES. THE CLOCK ALSO REPRESENTS THE RACE AGAINST TIME TO OPTIMIZE PERFORMANCE BEFORE THE EVENT.
